events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario: meter jaws burned/corroded on a commercial service entrance & metering installation, through its testing/inspection step. NEC Art. 230 governs service entrance conductors and equipment. Burned/corroded meter jaws indicate a defective connection, likely overheating — a serious hazard (fire/arc risk). This is non-compliant and defective. Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical. Work stage: tested/inspected. Percent complete: maybe 95. Conditions: installed-defective, degraded, failed. Findings and actions.

    "De-energize and lockout/tagout the service before any further work",
    "Replace burned/corroded meter socket jaws or the meter base assembly per manufacturer instructions",
    "Inspect upstream/downstream service conductors and terminations for thermal damage",
    "Re-inspect and re-test after replacement before energizing"
  ],
  "rationale": "Burned/corroded meter jaws are a defective, degraded condition at inspected service metering equipment. Under NEC Art. 230, service equipment must be suitable for the conditions and maintained in safe/operative condition; damaged/overheated meter jaws are non-compliant and create a critical arc/fire hazard, so the work fails inspection.",

  ]
